stories.title: ‘Morale bombing’ Moscow is not justified | Letter
stories.summary: Prof Christian Enemark reacts to Ukraine’s largest drone raid on Russia, calling on it to respect the innocence of all civilians The main target of Ukraine’s largest-ever drone attack on Moscow was apparently an oil refinery on the city’s edge ( Moscow oil refinery struck in Ukraine’s biggest air raid on city since start of war, 18 June ). However, it also caused some civilian injuries and damage to private property. It is possible that this other damage was entirely unintended, but it is reason

stories.title: A letter to Europe: Join us in celebrating America’s birthday
stories.summary: Andrew Puzder is the U.S. ambassador to the EU. Bill White is the U.S. ambassador to Belgium. The United States is about to celebrate the 250th anniversary of its founding. All U.S. missions in Brussels have been working diligently to ensure that this is a celebration worthy of the milestone. America’s cultural and historic ties with Europe run deep, and our relationships with Belgium, the EU and NATO embody those ties. We believe that makes Brussels an ideal spot for this celebration, and we ar

stories.title: Inside the EU’s latest plan to keep would-be members close
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The European Commission is drawing up plans to give EU candidate countries economic benefits before they join the bloc, as governments search for ways to speed up enlargement without lowering the bar for entry. The proposals form part of the Commission’s push for “gradual integration” — rewarding candidates with greater access to the EU as they deliver reforms while their membership bids are still being processed, according to two officials granted anonymity to discu

stories.title: Archbishop of Canterbury calls for end to Israeli occupation of Palestine
stories.summary: Sarah Mullally ends visit to region with call for two-state solution that allows Israelis and Palestinians to live in peace The archbishop of Canterbury has called for an end to the Israeli occupation of Palestine after a pilgrimage in which she met Palestinians attacked by settlers and others detained without trial. Sarah Mullally, the head of the Church of England, and Hosam Naoum, the Anglican archbishop of Jerusalem, issued a joint letter on Thursday urging Anglicans around the world to pres

stories.title: Ukraine, Poland smooth over WW2 dispute at Gdansk aid forum
stories.summary: Ukraine and its European allies are looking to encourage investment in Kyiv's post-war recovery. The first tranche of an EU loan has already arrived with more deals on the table. But relations with Poland are strained.

stories.title: Kapu Tiborra bíznák a magyar űrprogramot, saját űrügynökség és űrtörvény is jöhet
stories.summary: Tanács Zoltán szerint szuverenitásnak és Magyarország önálló gazdaságstratégiájának fontos eleme lehet az űrtevékenység és az űrkutatás, és nem szeretnénk összeszerelő üzem maradni.

stories.summary: Harmadfokú hőségriasztás lesz aznap, de 2022-ben még a mostaninál is melegebb volt.

stories.summary: Az ügyészség a lapnak nem cáfolta a dokumentum valódiságát, csak azt írták, hogy minden közlés veszélyezteti a nyomozás érdekeit. Megkérdeztük Orbán Viktort, hogy adott-e utasítást az aranykonvoj ügyében, de kitérő választ kaptunk.

stories.title: Napilapból hetilap: új főszerkesztővel vág neki története legnagyobb átalakulásának a Délmagyar

stories.title: Sári Zsolt: Azt vállaltuk, hogy felszabadítjuk a magyar kultúrát
stories.summary: Alkotmányba foglalná a művészet és a kultúra szabadságát, mielőbb rendezné az Iparművészeti Múzeum ügyét. Infrastruktúrát és forrásokat ígért kézivezérelt irányítás helyett. A közgyűjteményi és örökségvédelemért felelős államtitkár volt A falakon túl vendége.

stories.summary: Ezer milliárd forint elköltése után gyökeresen megváltozott volna a főváros utóbbi évtizedekben megszokott látképe, és ezeket a terveket senkivel nem egyeztették. Vitézy Dávid miniszter most bejelentette: felülvizsgálják a Hauszmann Program beruházásait, igyekeznek funkciót találni az 50-100 milliárdos épületeknek, köztük a Karmelitának is.

stories.title: Germany news: Major warship project to be scrapped
stories.summary: The Defense Ministry is dropping a multi-billion-euro project to build F126 frigates, media say. And train services have resumed after a communications glitch overnight halted Germany's rail network . DW has more.

stories.summary: Keir Starmer came to power without a strategic concept of post-Brexit Britain’s place in the world. His successor must not repeat that mistake Sir Keir Starmer had years in opposition to prepare for government. His likely successor, Andy Burnham, has weeks. Unlike the outgoing prime minister, Mr Burnham will bring past ministerial experience to the top job as well as lessons learned as the mayor of Greater Manchester. stories.title: Seal at Hungarian zoo brushes teeth
stories.summary: A seal at Nyíregyháza Zoo in Hungary brushes its teeth in the Arctic Ice World exhibit, showcasing daily care and animal enrichment activities.

stories.title: Poland, Ukraine: Dispute between two close allies deepens
stories.summary: Polish President Karol Nawrocki last week stripped Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy of Poland's highest state honor. How did it come to this? And what has it got to do with next year's election in Poland?

stories.title: A British woman was strangled in the UK – why did the US military try her case? – podcast
stories.summary: When the academic Sarah Steele was assaulted in England, she had no idea her case would end up in front of a US military court. Harry Davies explains why military judges and juries are ruling on crimes committed in the UK A little-known system in which US military personnel are tried through a court martial for alleged crimes committed in the UK is under growing scrutiny. One person who has been through that system is the academic Sarah Steele. Steele told the Guardian investigations corresponde

stories.title: Pope moves to clarify when wars are justified. Trump probably won’t like the answer.
stories.summary: ROME — In a move likely to further raise tensions with the administration of U.S. President Donald Trump, Pope Leo XIV will gather the world’s cardinals behind closed doors on Friday to hash out new guidelines on when war is justified. It’s a topic that has already fired a spat between the White House and the Vatican within the context of the Iran conflict. When Leo said the disciples of Christ were never on the side of those who “once wielded the sword and today drop bombs,” V

stories.title: The Guardian view on EU talks with the Taliban: selling out the rights of girls, women and other Afghans | Editorial
stories.summary: Five years after the fall of Kabul, European states are anxious to send migrants back – regardless of what it takes and what awaits them Days after Kabul fell to the Taliban in August 2021, the EU’s top diplomat stressed the need to protect women and girls. “Cooperation with any future Afghan government will be conditioned on … respect for the fundamental rights of all Afghans,” Josep Borrell pledged . The regime’s attack on women’s rights began immediately, and has only intensified. The Taliban

stories.title: Charities condemn ‘arrogant’ plans to house asylum seekers at former military sites
stories.summary: Planning permission has been sought for three additional military sites Home Office plans to use three more former military sites to house thousands of asylum seekers have been condemned as “arrogant”, “costly” and “a political fix” by refugee charities and local stakeholders. Planning permission is being sought to build “basic” accommodation at MOD Bicester in Oxfordshire, RAF Barnham in Suffolk and RAF Linton-on-Ouse in North Yorkshire, a statement said. These new sites could house 3,750 claim

stories.summary: Ukrainian strikes continue to shut down Russian oil refineries and starve Crimea of gasoline and power; Belarus relay stations silenced. What we know on day 1,584 Continue reading...

stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. stories.title: Sudan: Warnings mount over the risk of new mass atrocities
stories.summary: As Sudan's paramilitary Rapid Support Forces mass around the strategic city of el-Obeid, fears are growing of atrocities similar to those seen in Darfur's el-Fasher. Could sanctions still prevent bloodshed?

stories.title: US Supreme Court clears way for mass deportations of Haitians and Syrians
stories.summary: The 6-3 ruling hands Donald Trump a sweeping victory on immigration, stripping legal protections from hundreds of thousands of people the State Department warns would be returning to dangerous countries.

stories.title: Supreme Court allows Trump to end protected status for Haitian and Syrian immigrants
stories.summary: The ruling opens the path for the Trump administration to deport hundreds of thousands of immigrants who have been living in the US for years.

stories.title: Burnham’s dilemma: A free ride on Starmer’s majority, or risk an election to seek his own
stories.summary: LONDON — It is an unwritten rule in British politics that a new prime minister who takes over mid-term will soon want a general election to win a majority they can call their own.  That is the position Andy Burnham will find himself in, if, as expected, he succeeds Keir Starmer in three weeks’ time.  The calculation is never straightforward — and for Burnham, the dilemma is particularly acute. If he sticks with what he’s got, he will leave himself vulnerable to accusations that he is i

stories.title: Far-right German lawmaker faces scrutiny after photo appears to show Nazi salute
stories.summary: BERLIN — A senior lawmaker in the far-right Alternative for Germany party is under mounting pressure after a newly surfaced photograph appears to show him making a Nazi salute — an allegation he denies, insisting the gesture was intended as a joke. The previously unpublished image, obtained by POLITICO’s Inside AfD podcast , shows Martin Reichardt, a member of Germany’s federal parliament, raising his left arm while smiling during a gathering six years ago. While Reichardt denies the

stories.title: Oil price falls to pre-Iran war levels as more tankers exit strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Stock markets on both sides of Atlantic up as concerns ease over prospect of another inflationary shock Business live – latest updates Oil prices have fallen to pre-Iran war levels as more oil tankers exited the strait of Hormuz. Brent crude, the global benchmark, fell to a low of $72.24 a barrel on Thursday, slightly lower than the day before the US and Israel launched missile attacks on Tehran on 28 February. Prices have fallen more than 20% this month. Continue reading...

stories.title: Unions urge teachers to strike as French school exams go ahead in up to 40C heat
stories.summary: Claims health of staff and children at risk as France struggles to adapt heat-trap school buildings Teachers in France are risking their own and students’ health in overheated schools as a severe heatwave sets new record temperatures , education unions said, urging staff to strike over “unacceptable working conditions”. Several teaching unions on Thursday issued a joint statement denouncing a “blatant lack of preparation” by the government, after teachers have had to work in classrooms where tem

stories.title: City of Paris achieves partial victory over TotalEnergies in climate risks case
stories.summary: French capital hails ‘landmark decision’ against oil firm relating to disclosure of emissions from oil and gas products A Paris court has ​ruled that the French oil company TotalEnergies must disclose the climate risks linked to emissions from its oil and gas products and set out plans to ⁠address them in a high-stakes case brought by NGOs and the city of Paris. The ruling on Thursday is a partial victory for climate change NGOs seeking to apply France’s 2017 corporate duty of vigilance law to t

stories.title: JD Vance says Nixon’s Watergate scandal would be ‘12-hour news story’ today
stories.summary: Vice-president declares admiration for 37th president and claims Nixon and Trump are targeted by ‘deep state’ forces JD Vance does not think the era-defining Watergate scandal would have lasted more than a single news cycle in today’s fragmented, hyper-partisan political environment – and certainly would not have led to a president’s downfall. Speaking at the Richard Nixon presidential library in Yorba Linda, California, on Thursday to promote his new book, Communion, Vance discussed his spiritu

stories.title: California billionaire tax will appear on ballot after deadline for deal passes
stories.summary: Proposal to introduce one-time tax of 5% will go to voters in November after backers decline to withdraw measure California’s billionaire tax, explained: what you need to know California voters will get to decide in November whether billionaires should pay a one-time 5% tax, after a deadline passed on Thursday for its backers to withdraw the measure. “Enthusiasm for the billionaire tax is unlike anything we have seen before,” Debru Carthan, vice-president of the union sponsoring the measure, sai

stories.title: California’s proposed billionaire tax: what you need to know
stories.summary: Plan to levy 5% tax on California billionaires championed by progressives – but state’s super rich are forcefully opposed Full story: California billionaire tax heads to ballot The proposed billionaire tax in California is officially heading to voters’ ballots in November. After getting more than double the necessary signatures to qualify, the secretary of state certified the ballot measure late on Thursday. The confirmation came after backroom dealing didn’t pan out between California’s governo

stories.title: Xi Jinping has hosted more than a dozen leaders this year, as ‘middle powers’ look beyond the US
stories.summary: China’s leader wants to promote his alternative to the current world order, and his efforts are being assisted by a capricious US Xi Jinping meets Bangladesh’s new prime minister on Friday, the latest in a wave of world leaders to visit Beijing this year as the Chinese leader builds his influence and economic ties, and seeks to “shift the balance of power” away from the west. Xi’s meeting with Tarique Rahman comes less than two weeks after the Chinese leader welcomed Myanmar’s military chief-tur

stories.title: Rescue teams race to Venezuela amid fears thousands killed in earthquakes
stories.summary: US among countries sending help to search for survivors on north coast, where dozens of buildings flattened, as official death toll reaches 235 Rescue teams are racing to Venezuela’s shattered northern coast after almost simultaneous earthquakes reduced dozens of buildings to rubble, killing at least 235 people but with thousands more fatalities feared. The US secretary of state, Marco Rubio, said the defence department would help search and rescue teams deploy to the affected region after Venez

stories.title: Former Las Vegas youth pastor dies days after being charged in wife’s fatal fall
stories.summary: David Vander Meer was recently arrested for the death of his wife in national park 20 years ago after new information revealed A Las Vegas man and former youth pastor died dead days after he was arrested and charged with murdering his wife, who plummeted thousands of feet to her death while hiking in Utah ’s Zion national park in 2006. At a scheduled extradition hearing on Thursday, a Las Vegas judge announced that David Vander Meer was deceased, according to KSNV . Continue reading...

stories.title: Turkey journalists angered after NATO summit exclusion
stories.summary: Turkish outlets critical of Recep Tayyip Erdogan's government have been denied accreditation to cover the event in July. Press freedom groups called the development "alarming."

stories.title: Cargo ship hit by projectile in Hormuz as Iran issues renewed passage warning
stories.summary: The British military said a cargo ship on the new Omani UN-backed route sustained bridge damage from a projectile, hours after the IRGC warned that vessels without Iranian permission were transiting "illegally".

stories.title: Britain’s six prime ministers since 2016 – ranked!
stories.summary: From Cameron’s Brexit exit to Starmer’s Burnham bow-out, half a dozen PMs have gone. So who’s the best of the bunch? The UK has had six prime ministers in the last 10 years – with a seventh likely to be in place by as early as mid-July. John Crace ranks those who have been booted out of Downing Street between 2016 and 2026. Continue reading...

stories.title: US, Cuba and Iran join global rescue effort after Venezuela earthquakes
stories.summary: Latin American and European countries also offer solidarity and help to nation already enduring a humanitarian crisis Countries around the world, including Iran, the US and Cuba, have committed to help with rescue efforts in Venezuela after deadly twin earthquakes hit the country. On the evening of 24 June, Venezuela was rocked by two back-to-back earthquakes with a magnitude of 7.2 and 7.5, which killed at least 164 people, injured close to 1,000 more, and caused significant damage to the capit

stories.title: ‘Constitutional coup’ claims as Zimbabwe senate approves extending presidential term
stories.summary: Opposition figures fear changes will further tighten 83-year-old president Emmerson Mnangagwa’s hold on power Zimbabwe is on the brink of amending its constitution to give the president more time in office, a change that the government says will bring stability but that opponents have labelled a “constitutional coup”. The upper house of Zimbabwe’s parliament voted on Wednesday 75-4 in favour of the constitutional amendments, which will allow President Emmerson Mnangagwa to stay in office until 2

stories.title: ‘Paralysed by fear’: Venezuelans tell of escape and loss after huge earthquakes
stories.summary: People in Caracas and coastal towns describe powerful quakes that collapsed buildings and killed at least 164 As a double whammy of powerful earthquakes rattled Venezuela’s northern coast on Wednesday, residents of the capital, Caracas, scrambled out on to the streets from shuddering, fractured buildings. “It was horrible. I felt like the house was moving to a different rhythm to the earth. I had to carry my mum out. She was paralysed by fear,” said 18-year-old Sebastian Rodríguez, whose family

stories.title: Israeli forces arrest Palestinian ‘doctor of the poor’
stories.summary: Dr Mazen Al-Rantisi, a 71-year-old physician well known for providing care to low-income Palestinians, was arrested in the occupied West Bank Israeli forces on Sunday arrested a prominent 71-year-old Palestinian physician known as the “doctor of the poor” in a pre-dawn raid on his home in the occupied West Bank, prompting widespread condemnation. Dr Mazen Al-Rantisi, a physician widely known for providing care to low-income Palestinians, was arrested in the al-Tira neighbourhood of Ramallah. Con

stories.title: Iran rejects UN-backed plan to free ships trapped in strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Blocking of proposal backed by Oman signals new threat to free passage through strait vital to world economy Iran has rejected UN-backed plans for the mass evacuation of ships through the strait of Hormuz, creating a new threat to the free passage of commercial ships through the strait. The proposal, backed by Oman, was potentially the first phase of a broader Omani proposal to consult on setting up a new management of the strait based on voluntary fees and modelled on the Malacca and Singapore

stories.title: New evidence casts doubt on RFK Jr testimony before Senate
stories.summary: Kennedy repeatedly said 2019 Samoa trip had ‘nothing to do with vaccines’. stories.title: Karl Stefanovic to leave Nine immediately after Today host’s podcast controversy

stories.title: King Charles’s tax bill: what did we learn, and what is still in the dark?
stories.summary: We know the monarch paid £24.6m in tax over the last two years, but we still don’t know how wealthy he actually is Crown estate makes more than £1bn profit for third year running King and Queen will not live at Buckingham Palace after £369m refit King Charles has become Britain’s first monarch in modern times to reveal how much tax he pays on his private income: £24.6m over the last two years. It’s a move celebrated by some as heralding an era of greater transparency from the monarchy. But just

stories.title: King and Queen will not live at Buckingham Palace after £369m refit
stories.summary: Charles and Camilla to remain at Clarence House and are said to want the public to have more access to ‘monarchy HQ’ King Charles’s tax bill: what did we learn and what is still in the dark? Crown estate makes more than £1bn profit for third year running King Charles and Queen Camilla will not move into Buckingham Palace when £369m of buildings works to update it finish next year, preferring to remain at Clarence House, their London home nearby. The announcement came as it was revealed the king

stories.title: UN agency pauses ship evacuations through strait of Hormuz after vessel struck
stories.summary: International Maritime Organization says safety guarantees must be confirmed before ships can move again A United Nations agency has paused the evacuation of ships through the strait of Hormuz after the British military said a vessel was hit by a projectile off the coast of Oman following the passage of several tankers that used a route backed by the UN. The head of the UN’s International Maritime Organization said on Thursday that the plan to move stranded ships out of the Persian Gulf through

stories.title: Major wildfire rips through moorland close to Greater Manchester
stories.summary: Blaze on Tintwistle Moor, Glossop, has burned more than 500 square metres, forcing road closures and smoke warning A major wildfire close to Greater Manchester has torn through large areas of moorland for more than 24 hours, forcing road closures and smoke warnings as fire crews battle to bring it under control. The blaze, on Tintwistle Moor, near Glossop, has burned an area of more than 500 square metres of moorland and woodland, with thick plumes of smoke directly affecting the villages of Tin

stories.title: Experts say three recent powerful earthquakes are not related
stories.summary: 5.6-magnitude California tremor, 7.2-magnitude temblor in Japan and two quakes in Venezuela all struck within hours A 5.6-magnitude earthquake struck a rural part of northern California on Wednesday. Hours later, a 7.2-magnitude earthquake hit the northern coast of Japan and two powerful earthquakes rocked Venezuela in a devastating mass casualty event. The tremors happened in the span of eight hours, prompting online speculation over whether they were related. Continue reading...

stories.title: Paris to ban drinking alcohol in public as hospitals hit heatwave breaking point
stories.summary: Ban kicks in at noon on Friday as police chief says hospitals in the French capital have reached ‘saturation point’ Parisians will ⁠be temporarily banned ​from drinking alcohol in public as hospitals in the capital buckle under a deadly heatwave ‌gripping France ‌and much of Europe . “We are reaching a saturation point in hospital facilities,” the head of Paris police, Patrice Faure, said on Thursday. He warned the new measures, which include a ban on alcohol takeaway sales, were needed to stem

stories.title: Reform UK plan to target EU nationals based in Britain ‘absolutely outrageous’
stories.summary: Exclusive: Rights group says Nigel Farage’s party is reneging on promises made during the Brexit referendum campaign UK politics live – latest updates EU nationals based permanently in the UK have expressed alarm over a Reform UK plan to target their rights to accommodation and employment, saying the policy is a betrayal of promises made in the Brexit referendum 10 years ago. Under updated migration policies, Nigel Farage’s party would evict all overseas nationals from social housing and make it

stories.title: Russia used Israeli firm’s tool to crack phone months after ties severed, report finds
stories.summary: Case of Andrei Pivovarov raises questions about how much control Cellebrite has over its own software Russian authorities used tools from the Israeli company Cellebrite to break into the phone of a political prisoner, months after the company said it cancelled its contracts with Russia, an investigation by the University of Toronto’s Citizen Lab research unit has found . The case raises questions about how much control Cellebrite has over its own software, which allows users to easily break into

stories.title: Banning social media for teens: solution or overreaction?
stories.summary: One in seven teens in the EU spends more than eight hours a day in front of screens, a new study shows. Are social media bans the answer?

stories.title: Germany considers 'opt-out' organ donor option
stories.summary: Germany is one of few European countries that still has an exclusively "opt-in" choice for organ donation — despite long waiting lists for organs. Now, parliament is debating a change.

stories.title: Iran: UK Navy reports cargo vessel hit in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: The reported attack has caused the UN maritime agency to halt its evacuations of seafarers stranded in the Gulf. Meanwhile, Rubio is finishing up his Mideast tour. Follow DW for more.

stories.title: Latin America: Why are voters turning to the right?
stories.summary: Far-right populists seem to be on the rise in Latin America after defeating leftist candidates in a number of recent presidential elections. Why are they so popular? And are they here to stay?

stories.title: Young people avoiding the news, Reuters report
stories.summary: An alarming number of young people are turning away from the news, according to the Reuters Digital News Report presented at DW's Global Media Forum. Despite this, the report's author says, "journalism still matters."

stories.title: US Supreme Court sides with Bayer over Roundup cancer suits
stories.summary: Bayer, which acquired Roundup in 2018, said the decision is good for science and farmers. The Supreme Court's ruling was met with backlash from both Republicans and Democrats.

stories.title: Germany poor in loss to Ecuador at 2026 World Cup
stories.summary: Germany had already qualified for the knockouts of the 2026 World Cup, but their performance was still disappointing in a 2-1 defeat.

stories.title: King Charles won’t live in Buckingham Palace, reveals taxes
stories.summary: Buckingham Palace has been the British monarch’s official residence in London for nearly two centuries. But royal officials say King Charles and Queen Camilla won’t reside there.

stories.title: Venezuela: 235 dead in devastating, back-to-back earthquakes
stories.summary: Venezuela's health minister said at least 235 people have died after two strong earthquakes rattled the capital and surrounding regions. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Why is Germany taking a big stake in a major tankmaker?
stories.summary: The German government will buy a 40% stake in tankmaker KNDS. The deal is as much about control in the critical defense sector as it is about the Franco-German relationship.

stories.title: Fact check: No, Zelenskyy wasn't killed in a Russian airstrike
stories.summary: Amid a flare-up in violence between Russia and Ukraine, viral posts claim Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has died. DW Fact check takes a closer look.

stories.title: Oil price falls back to pre-Iran war levels
stories.summary: Signs that traffic through the key Strait of Hormuz shipping route is gradually resuming has helped to push the oil price down.

stories.title: Families lay flowers on barbed wire barricade on anniversary of deadly Kenya protests
stories.summary: Protesters demand justice for the dozens killed during the 2024 demonstrations and last year's anniversary protests.

stories.title: Could a Madison Square Garden wedding be the love story of Taylor Swift's wildest dreams?
stories.summary: Officials confirmed someone applied for a permit to close the streets near Madison Square Garden on 4 July.

stories.title: Women alleging rape and sexual assault in France call to abolish statute of limitations
stories.summary: More than 50 women are calling for the abolition of the statute of limitations which they say has prevented them seeking justice.

stories.title: A Chinese box office hit sparks a debate about identity in Singapore
stories.summary: A nostalgic tale about family, hope and hardship has opened an unexpected conversation.

stories.title: The abundant but expensive energy source that's under your feet
stories.summary: Start-ups are taking fresh approaches to geothermal energy, but will the economics work?

stories.title: Why the trail went cold in Nancy Guthrie case
stories.summary: The lack of a breakthrough in the abduction of a famous TV anchor's mother is puzzling given the huge publicity.

stories.title: Paris restricts alcohol consumption and sales as Europe's heatwave shifts east
stories.summary: Temperatures in Germany could hit 40C in some areas while French officials bring in alcohol restrictions to ease pressure on hospitals.

stories.title: UN pauses Strait of Hormuz evacuation plan after cargo ship attacked
stories.summary: The ship was reportedly struck by an "unknown projectile" near Oman but no casualties were reported.

stories.title: Rescues and prayers a day after Venezuelan earthquakes
stories.summary: The BBC's Vanessa Silva reports from Caracas as the city faces the aftermath of back-to-back tremors.

stories.title: Rescuers search rubble for survivors as Venezuela earthquakes kill at least 235
stories.summary: Nearly 1,500 people have been injured after two seven-plus magnitude shallow quakes hit seconds apart.

stories.title: Earthquake is devastating blow to Venezuela at time of uncertainty
stories.summary: The twin quakes come less than six months after Venezuela's then-leader Nicolás Maduro was seized by US forces.

stories.title: Two years after Gen Z uprising, Kenyan protesters return to Nairobi streets
stories.summary: Two years after Kenya's 2024 Gen Z tax revolt shook the nation, protesters gathered in Nairobi on 25 June to seek justice for victims and press for greater government accountability.

stories.title: Does being a good mayor make you a good prime minister?
stories.summary: The Greater Manchester mayoralty made Andy Burnham famous. Now he’s hoping he can take the approach he developed in the city to the whole country. This week on Westminster Insider, host Sascha O’Sullivan speaks to four serving mayors — and one former one about whether the role will prepare Burnham for the highest office in the land. Labour Mayor of the East Midlands Claire Ward, who was a minister in Gordon Brown’s government, told Sascha that mayors have much more power than j

stories.title: What Keir Starmer's resignation means for European security
stories.summary: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer was widely known for his support of Ukraine and his efforts towards a new EU–UK defense partnership. What will happen to those policies now that Starmer has announced his resignation?

stories.title: Biggest city in Crimea without power after Ukraine strikes
stories.summary: Sevastopol's Moscow-installed governor warns there will be no electricity in some areas until the evening.

stories.title: Papua New Guinea at risk of food shortages as El Niño brings frost and drought
stories.summary: Oxfam predicts PNG will be worst-hit country in Pacific from the weather pattern, with up to 3 million people affected nationwide Families across Papua New Guinea’s Highlands are facing depleted harvests and the threat of hunger after the El Niño weather pattern brought frost and prolonged dry conditions that have destroyed food gardens providing sustenance and income for thousands of households. The effects of El Niño emerged in recent weeks, bringing drought conditions, falling water levels an

stories.title: Stubborn, arrogant, a genius: France’s De Gaulle epic shows up the tepidity of our politics | Alexander Hurst
stories.summary: The man who led Free France during Nazi occupation was single-minded to the point of obstinacy. But he believed in his own power to make history How much of our political agency have we sacrificed on the altar of imagined constraints? That question has been troubling me since last week, when I stepped out of the glitteringly art deco Grand Rex cinema in Paris. I had just been to see part one of La Bataille de Gaulle, a two-part epic based on British historian Julian Jackson’s extraordinary biogr

stories.title: Why Germans don't have air conditioning
stories.summary: Unlike the US or parts of Asia, many homes in Germany and northern Europe aren't equipped to deal with extreme heat. But as the world gets hotter, trends are changing.

stories.title: How countries live with earthquakes and build resilience
stories.summary: Earthquakes are a fact of nature, but living with earthquakes doesn't have to mean living in fear. DW takes a closer look at some countries showing how preparation, innovation and determination can save lives.

stories.title: India braces for drought as El Nino looms
stories.summary: Officials and farmers in India are preparing for what meteorologists say could be the driest monsoon season in a decade.

stories.title: Germany news: Deutsche Bahn advises against travel amid heat
stories.summary: Germany's national rail operator is allowing passengers to cancel trips because of the heat. Meanwhile, Germany lost 2-1 to Ecuador at the World Cup, but both teams will advance to the next round.

stories.title: Europe heat wave: No respite as extreme heat continues
stories.summary: Some 101 million people in Europe will likely see temperatures above 35 C on Thursday, an analysis shows. And Germany is expecting things to warm up even more. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Family seeks justice after Indian sailor killed in US strike
stories.summary: After a US strike on a commercial vessel in the Gulf of Oman killed an Indian sailor, there are urgent questions about seafarer safety, accountability and India's response. DW spoke with the sailor's family.

stories.title: Human rights expert Philippe Sands wins German Peace Prize
stories.summary: From advocating for the law of ecocide to representing Palestinian rights, the descendant of Holocaust survivors tirelessly fights against genocide and crimes against humanity.

stories.title: Germany: Racism is almost a daily reality for Muslims
stories.summary: Insults, discrimination, violence, deaths: Experts warn of growing anti-Muslim racism in Germany — and criticize policymakers.

stories.title: The 'Flamingo Revolution': What's behind Albania's protests?
stories.summary: For weeks now, images of crowds of Albanians protesting on the streets have been relayed around the world. The protesters say they are fighting for democracy. PM Edi Rama insists the movement is part of a hybrid war.

stories.title: Independent MPs launch new Australian centrist party
stories.summary: Zali Steggall and Allegra Spender say Community Strong Australia will offer citizens 'reason over rage'.

stories.title: Anthropic accuses Chinese rival Alibaba of illicitly extracting AI capabilities
stories.summary: The firm alleged that Alibaba used fraudulent accounts to access data from its Claude AI model.

stories.title: Hormuz confusion deepens as Iran and US clash over who controls it
stories.summary: Trump said Tehran had promised no tolls or charges in the strait, hours after Iran and Oman announced a joint permit system asserting their authority over the waterway. Rubio flew to Abu Dhabi to reassure Gulf allies, as analysts warned control over Hormuz would be a major strategic victory for Iran

stories.title: At least 164 killed and 971 injured in powerful Venezuela earthquakes
stories.summary: Residents of the capital Caracas were forced to evacuate their residences and seek shelter outdoors as the quakes, which struck less than 200 km from the capital, caused widespread damage.

stories.title: Mumbai monsoon floods streets and disrupts travel
stories.summary: Heavy monsoon rains flood Mumbai, submerging roads, disrupting traffic and forcing residents to wade through waterlogged streets in India’s financial hub.

stories.title: Trump says 'courageous' Zelenskyy is 'doing pretty well' in war with Russia
stories.summary: "You have to say he's courageous, he's got great equipment, he's got great men, he's got fighters," Trump said of Zelenskyy.

stories.title: Moment earthquake hits Venezuela, sending people fleeing in panic
stories.summary: Images showing cracked walls, collapsed ceiling panels and debris strewn across parts of Simón Bolívar International Airport highlighted the scale of damage after two powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela on 24 June 2026.

stories.title: Heatwave scorches Europe from London to Madrid as temperatures near 39C
stories.summary: A powerful heatwave swept across Europe, stretching from France to Spain and Belgium and pushing temperatures towards 39C, raising concerns about public safety and pressure on infrastructure.

stories.title: North Korean soldier suspected of defecting crosses demilitarised zone into South Korea
stories.summary: According to data from South Korea's Ministry of Unification, more than 34,000 North Koreans have defected to the South since its records began in 1998.

stories.title: Trump asks Congress for billions for Iran war, after tension with Republicans
stories.summary: But the budget faces an uphill battle as the president spars with some members of his own party over the issue.

stories.title: IBM hails new 'block of flats' design breakthrough for ultra tiny chips
stories.summary: IBM says it has created the world's first known chip tech below 1 nanometre - but it will be some time before it's ready for production.

stories.title: International community pledges aid to Venezuela after devastating earthquakes
stories.summary: The United States Geological Survey has estimated that the final death toll could be between 10,000 and 100,000 people.

stories.title: People flee as building collapses on Caracas outskirts
stories.summary: The capital and its surrounding areas have seen some of the worst damage from the 7.2 and 7.5 quakes, which hit a minute apart.

stories.title: Ryanair says it will reluctantly not charge parents to sit next to children
stories.summary: The airline had typically charged adults a fee of £8 each way to sit with their young children.

stories.title: IRGC warns ships off Oman's corridor in Hormuz as Rubio draws line on tolls
stories.summary: The IRGC warned that any vessel navigating outside Tehran's designated routes was acting illegally, directly rejecting Oman's newly announced temporary corridor, as Rubio told a GCC meeting the US would not accept fees regardless of how they are framed.

stories.title: Moment French navy boards suspected Russian 'ghost fleet' tanker
stories.summary: The French navy boarded a tanker suspected of belonging to Russia's so-called "ghost fleet" in the Mediterranean, the fifth such operation since September 2025, as Paris steps up efforts to enforce sanctions linked to the war in Ukraine.

stories.title: Laissons Parler Les Gens: Podcast addresses issues facing young Africans online
stories.summary: Youssoupha acknowledged cyberbullying and harassment were part of the challenges facing young Africans today, but he urged the youth to focus on the positives.

stories.title: Key figure in South Africa police corruption scandal pleads guilty
stories.summary: Prosecutors say Vusimusi "Cat" Matlala could provide evidence against "high-ranking officials".

stories.title: La Guaira, Venezuela’s port city, shows extensive earthquake damage after the quake
stories.summary: New images from La Guaira on 25 June revealed widespread destruction after twin earthquakes struck Venezuela's Caribbean coast, damaging buildings, roads and key public infrastructure.

stories.title: Nincs bajom a medencékkel, de aszálykor ne azok kapjanak elsőbbséget, mondta az EP agrárbizottságának alelnöke
stories.summary: Az öntözésnek és a kockázatkezelésnek fontosabb szerepe lesz az éghajlatváltozás miatt az EU közös mezőgazdasági politikájában, hangsúlyozta Norbert Lins.

stories.title: Az MNB szerint nem dobná meg nagyon az inflációt, ha kivezetnék az árrésstopot
stories.summary: A jegybank inflációs jelentése szerint az élelmiszerláncok már alkalmazkodtak az árrésstophoz, így annak kivezetése alig növelné az árakat.

stories.title: Lekerült Fenyő Miklós szobrának terve a XIII. kerület napirendjéről
stories.summary: Az önkormányzat szerint vita alakult ki a szobor terve körül.

stories.title: Vb-meccsgyőzelmet ünneplő tömegbe hajtott egy autós Mexikóban
stories.summary: Tizenheten sérültek meg az esetben.

stories.title: Összelegózott a gyerek egy vb-trófeát, ékszerész apja erre gyémántokkal ékesítette fel
stories.summary: Egy dubaji mester 900 ékkővel és arannyal díjazta gyereke kreativitását.

stories.title: Vegyi anyag szivárgott a CATL debreceni üzemében, belső vizsgálat indult
stories.summary: Nagyjából egy liter dietil-karbonát került ki egy tárolóból, a cég szerint a szakemberek 5 percen belül elhárították a problémát.

stories.title: Olyan füst volt, hogy nem láttunk semmit, amikor pedig leértünk, horrorfilmbe illő látvány tárult elénk
stories.summary: Helyiek beszéltek arról, mit éltek át, amikor egy percen belül két nagy erejű földrengés rázta meg Venezuela északi részét. Volt, aki a zuhany alól szaladt ki törölközőben az utcára. Eddig több mint 180 halálos áldozatot és közel ezer sérültet erősítettek meg.

stories.title: Az egyik boszniai védő már az amerikai csapat kiejtésére pályázik
stories.summary: Stjepan Radeljić elismerte, az USA csapata elég jó, de azért nem szerénykedett az esélyekkel.

stories.title: Kisebb visszamenőleges béremelést kaptak a pedagógusok
stories.summary: Ez havi néhány ezer forintos emelést jelent a gyakorlatban.

stories.title: Lebunkózták a skót edzőt, aki nem volt hajlandó normálisan válaszolni egy riporternek
stories.summary: Steve Clarke-ot már túlzottan óvatos taktikája miatt is érte kritika.

stories.title: Európa keleti fele is bőven kap majd a most nyugaton tomboló hőhullámból
stories.summary: A német állami vasút arra kéri az utasokat, mondják le az utazásaikat.

stories.title: Kereskedelmi békejobbot nyújtottak az uniós kormányok Trumpnak
stories.summary: Az uniós miniszterek Tanácsa megszavazta az EU és az USA közötti vámalkut végrehajtó jogszabályokat, valamint a Boeing és az Airbus vitájában betárazott büntetővámok további jegelését.

stories.title: Megemelte az iPad és a MacBook árát az Apple
stories.summary: A cég szerint az áremelkedés oka a mikrochipek drágulása.

stories.title: Hajón úsztatott plakáton, pixelesen mutatta meg a Nike, milyen lesz az új német mez
stories.summary: Jamal Musiala feszít a fehér mezben, amiből keveset lehet kisilabizálni, és már nem az Adidas gyártja.

stories.title: Ecuador–Németország 2-1
stories.summary: Sané már két perc után vezetést szerzett, de Ecuador a 78. percre már megfordította a meccset.

stories.title: Curaçao–Elefántcsontpart
stories.summary: Az elefántcsontparti Nicolas Pépé két kapuralövéséből kettő gólt lőtt a vb legkisebb csapatának.

stories.title: Forsthoffer Ágnes bekeményít, listázzák a hiányzásokat a parlamentben
stories.summary: A házelnök egyelőre nem büntetett meg senkit, de listába rendezték a parlament honlapján, hogy ki hányszor nem volt ott a szavazásokon. A csúcstartó: Lázár János.

stories.title: Felfüggesztette az ENSZ a Hormuzi-szorosban rekedt tengerészek kimentését
stories.summary: Egy hajót támadás ért, ezért ideiglenesen leállították az evakuációt.

stories.summary: Az ukrán elnöknek ezzel az a célja, hogy nyomást gyakoroljon az oroszokra a háború befejezésére.

stories.summary: Ecuador a győzelembe menekült, 2-1-re nyert utolsó csoportmeccsén a már korábban továbbjutott németek ellen.

stories.summary: Afrikai-karibi rangadóval zárult az E csoport a foci-vb-n, Elefántcsontpart továbbjutott a legjobb 32 közé.

Törökország–Egyesült Államok 2-1
Paraguay–Ausztrália 0-0

stories.summary: A gazdasági és energetikai miniszter szerint jobb, ha csak este kilenc óra után töltjük a laptopot, a telefont és az elektromos autót is.

stories.title: Pakistan police rescue French woman and children allegedly held captive by husband for 12 years
stories.summary: Husband arrested after Sylvie Yasmina, 54, and five children found at home in north-western province Pakistan police say they have rescued a French woman and her five children after she told authorities she had been held captive by her husband for more than a decade and subjected to years of domestic abuse in the country’s north-west. The woman, identified as 54-year-old Sylvie Yasmina, was rescued earlier this week from a mud-brick home in Bara, a town in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province near the Af

stories.title: UK prioritised ties with UAE over averting mass atrocities in Sudan, MPs to be told
stories.summary: Foreign Office failed to act on warnings of genocide due to ‘pressure’ from emirates, Yale human rights investigator will tell a parliamentary select committee The British government had received intelligence that Ethiopia appeared to be supporting a genocidal militia in Sudan’s civil war as far back as 2024 but did not go public with the news for fear of upsetting the United Arab Emirates (UAE), a parliamentary committee will hear. In May 2024, officials from the Foreign, Commonwealth and Devel

stories.title: Trump offers Iran an economic lifeline — with strings
stories.summary: Iran has seen a speedy rebound in oil exports after the US-Iran deal granted immediate waivers on US sanctions. Relief on frozen assets and a $300 billion reconstruction fund now hinge on Tehran meeting key conditions.

stories.title: Ukraine says major Crimea bridge destroyed in latest attack
stories.summary: Ukraine continues to hit infrastructure on the Crimea to isolate the Russian-annexed peninsula. Meanwhile, Russia attacked Volodymyr Zelenskyy's hometown with a ballistic missile in a deadly strike.

stories.title: The ICE raids are over, but some Minnesotans say they still live in fear
stories.summary: Months after the Trump administration's immigration crackdown officially ended, Minneapolis is still feeling the impact.

stories.title: Kenyan minister orders halt to construction of US Ebola facility
stories.summary: Decision comes after Aden Duale was held in contempt for ignoring previous high court ruling to stop work Kenya’s health minister told a court he had ordered preparations for a US-run Ebola quarantine facility to stop, after being held in contempt for ignoring a previous order to end work. Many Kenyans strongly oppose the facility , with deadly protests erupting since the complex was announced in May for US citizens evacuated from the Democratic Republic of Congo, which is grappling with a wides

stories.title: Turkey arrests more than 200 in crackdown before NATO summit
stories.summary: The Ankara prosecutor's office said 209 people were detained, while 32 others remained at large. Turkey has announced a ban on demonstrations amid preparations for next month's NATO summit in the capital.

stories.title: Kenya orders immediate halt to controversial US-backed Ebola facility
stories.summary: The project had sparked court proceedings, public protests and questions about the country's role in responding to an outbreak beyond its borders.

stories.title: Play puts spotlight on Kenya’s crisis of gender-based violence
stories.summary: Autobiographical work Free Me aims to encourage victims to speak out in country where violence against women is rising There are audible gasps in the auditorium in Nairobi as a husband launches a volley of blows and slaps on his wife and pushes her to the floor. “I wish I could spare you this,” the wife tells the audience. “My husband beat me up as if we were in a bar fight. Except, in a bar someone fights back.” The scene comes from Free Me, an autobiographical play by Gathoni Kimuyu, a Kenyan

stories.title: Dettol apologises after ‘toxic men’ advert sparks backlash in China
stories.summary: British disinfectant brand withdraws advert about a man’s efforts to find a ‘clean and untouched’ woman The British hygiene brand Dettol has apologised after an advertisement released in China, which it said was intended to criticise “toxic men”, was widely condemned on social media as offensive to women. The five-minute advert for a multipurpose disinfectant, released across many online platforms at the end of May, features a man comparing his girlfriend with his former partner. Learning that h

stories.title: Philippines blocks GoreBox gaming app after school shooting kills three
stories.summary: Investigators says one of two teenagers who allegedly opened fire on students at high school was regular player Philippine authorities have temporarily blocked the online gaming app GoreBox days after a rare school shooting in the south-east Asian country killed three students and injured 20 others. Investigators said that one of the two teenagers accused of opening fire on students at San Jose National high school in Tacloban city had regularly played the game, which allows players to use vario

stories.title: France confirms first Ebola case in doctor who had worked in DRC
stories.summary: French health ministry says patient’s contacts are being traced and that risk to European public is very low The first case of Ebola has been confirmed in France, the country’s health ministry has said, in a doctor who had returned from a humanitarian mission to an area affected by the outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo. The patient was transferred to a specialist facility and was in a stable condition, the ministry said in a statement . “All precautionary measures, including the p

stories.title: Exploit last North Sea oil and gas or risk mass job losses, Andy Burnham urged
stories.summary: British Chamber of Commerce boss Shevaun Haviland says transition to clean energy could be handled better Andy Burnham should be ready to exploit the UK’s remaining North Sea oil and gas resources to avoid mass job losses in Scotland and the north-east, the director of the British Chambers of Commerce (BCC), Shevaun Haviland, has said. The decision about whether to allow extraction at the Jackdaw and Rosebank fields now appears likely to fall to a Burnham administration. Continue reading...

stories.title: Amnesty: A transz emberek Ruszin-Szendi állításával szemben nincsenek összezavarodva
stories.summary: A jogvédő szervezet szerint a honvédelmi miniszter kirekesztően beszélt a transzneműekről.

stories.title: Milliós fizetések, százmilliók propagandára, így égette a pénzt a Megafon és a Mandiner
stories.summary: A Mandiner saját beszámolójában is szerepel a propaganda szó, de a dokumentumból az is kiderül, hogy készültek a „reorganizációra”.

stories.summary: Már el is fogadta az erről szóló javaslatot a törvényalkotási bizottság.

stories.title: Bomló holttesteket találtak egy brit kórházban
stories.summary: Egy vizsgálat szerint nagy veszélye volt annak is, hogy gyászolók más emberek maradványait kapták meg.

stories.title: Jürgen Klopp úgy felpaprikázta magát egy kérdésen, hogy otthagyta az újságírókat
stories.summary: Sebastian Schweinsteiger ellentmondásos elefántcsontparti jellemzéséről akarták kérdezni a szintén szakértősködő Kloppot.

stories.title: Why Austrian author Ingeborg Bachmann remains a literary icon
stories.summary: A new documentary featuring Sandra Hüller celebrates the Austrian poet and author who was a literary star of her era. She would have turned 100 this year.

stories.title: Climate change: Heat wave costs the German economy billions
stories.summary: Never before has a June been as hot in Germany as in 2026. This has consequences not only for people and nature, but also for businesses. Heat reduces productivity and causes illness, while cooling is expensive.

stories.title: European heat wave is not normal summer weather
stories.summary: Human-induced climate change has intensified temperatures, making this year's record heat wave up to 4 degrees Celsius hotter.

stories.title: Heat wave: Why you should skip that cold beer or cocktail
stories.summary: Summer, the FIFA World Cup and a cold beer. Sounds like a dream? Be careful, though; the combination of heat and alcohol can quickly take a very bad turn.

stories.title: How Big Tech is changing journalism
stories.summary: Technology companies are shaping how news is presented and digested, leaving media outlets little option but to comply. At DW's Global Media Forum in Bonn, participants discussed how to resist and adapt.

stories.title: New giraffe born in Denmark zoo
stories.summary: At Copenhagen Zoo, a giraffe calf is born as staff and visitors witness the moment. The mother immediately cares for her newborn.

stories.title: Toronto streets fill with Panama and Croatia supporters
stories.summary: Supporters of Panama and Croatia turned downtown Toronto into a sea of flags and chants ahead of their crucial World Cup Group L clash.

stories.title: IAEA chief says inspections of Iran's nuclear sites 'going to happen'
stories.summary: IAEA's Grossi pushed back against Tehran's claim that no agreement has been reached to allow inspectors to inspect bombed enrichment sites, citing the signed US-Iran framework deal.

stories.title: Dead tree painted red to highlight climate change impact
stories.summary: A painted red oak at Kew Gardens, killed in the 2022 heatwave, stands beside a resilient “Tree of Hope”, highlighting climate change impacts in the UK.

stories.title: Kazakhstan signs €10bn in deals with Europe as Tokayev wraps Brussels visit
stories.summary: During President Kassym-Jomart Tokayev's visit to Brussels, strategic documents were signed with the EU, and 30 commercial deals were concluded with EU companies, with the EU being the Central Asian country's largest trading and investment partner.

stories.title: Giesinger celebrates Munich beer culture, pushes for Oktoberfest place
stories.summary: Munich Brewers’ Day 2026 brings processions, ceremony and beer celebrations to Marienplatz and Viktualienmarkt, honouring the city’s brewing guild tradition.

stories.title: Evo Morales warns of civil war from Chapare stronghold: 'I will not surrender'
stories.summary: Evo Morales speaks out from hiding in the Chapare jungle as Bolivia enters its seventh week of fuel, food and medicine shortages.

stories.title: Clean sweep for Mamdani-backed candidates in New York's Democratic primary
stories.summary: Brad Lander unseats Dan Goldman in a race that laid bare the city's divisions over the Israel-Gaza war.

stories.title: Air conditioning creates political divide after France records hottest day
stories.summary: France is being forced to re-think its longstanding reservations about air-conditioning as temperatures soar.

stories.title: Tehran labels framework deal 'US defeat' as Trump threatens to halt talks
stories.summary: Iranian key negotiator hailed the US–Iran framework deal as a “declaration of US defeat,” while Trump warned that talks would end immediately if reports that Tehran is seeking to impose tolls on ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz prove to be true.

stories.title: First whales killed as Iceland restarts commercial hunt
stories.summary: Iceland resumes commercial whaling after a two-year pause, with two fin whales processed at the Hvalur facility, reigniting controversy and protests.

stories.title: Portugal's World Cup victory sparks celebrations in Lisbon
stories.summary: Thousands of fans in Lisbon celebrated Portugal's 5-0 World Cup victory over Uzbekistan, with Cristiano Ronaldo scoring twice and becoming the first player to score in six different World Cup tournaments.

stories.title: IMF to closely monitor China’s economic growth amid trade tensions
stories.summary: An accurate measurement of China's digital economy is a priority for other powers competing with Beijing in fast-developing technologies.

stories.title: UN nuclear chief says inspectors will visit Iran sites as part of war deal
stories.summary: Rafael Grossi says the IAEA is "working on modalities" but an Iranian minister says such access would only be part of a final deal with the US.

stories.title: Catholics walk barefoot in mud ritual
stories.summary: Devotees in rice paddies cover themselves in mud and banana leaves for the Taong Putik festival, honouring St. John the Baptist with barefoot processions.

stories.title: Philippines bans video game played by alleged high school shooter
stories.summary: The rare school shooting that left three students killed and 20 others injured has shocked the country.

stories.title: Watch: Moment earthquakes hit airport and buildings in Caracas
stories.summary: Video filmed by BBC reporters, contributors and locals shows the aftermath, as frantic residents evacuate.

stories.title: This Indian state is trying to ensure no one grows old alone
stories.summary: Kerala is searching for new ways to care for its ageing population as migration leaves more elderly people living alone.

stories.title: Keir Starmer and Andy Burnham have met away from No 10 to discuss transition – as it happened
stories.summary: Prime minister said to have left Downing Street for secret meeting with his probable successor Burnham adviser calls for billions of pounds in borrowing for infrastructure Peter Walker is a senior Guardian political correspondent. The Liberal Democrats are marking the tenth anniversary of Brexit by enjoying their favourite pursuit – being rude about Nigel Farage. Nigel Farage pocketed a £5m “reward” for the damage he’s caused, while the rest of us are paying for it dearly. When he promised we wo

stories.title: ‘Is rejoining the EU inevitable?’ - Rafael Behr answered your questions on Brexit and more
stories.summary: It’s 10 years since the Brexit vote – and it’s also another one of those weeks in British politics … you asked our political columnist questions about a Burnham government, rejoining the EU, and more – read the Q&A below DyvimTvar asks: How do you think Burnham will deal with Trump? More of the same or will he be looking at the likely result of the midterm elections in the US? Raf: Burnham’s foreign policy is very mysterious. He very rarely talks about the world beyond British shores, and ha

stories.title: Von der Leyen to visit Pashinyan in Yerevan to build on Armenia’s pro-EU vote
stories.summary: As the EU rolls out a support package for Armenia, European Commission President von der Leyen is set to meet re-elected Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan in Yerevan after he secured a decisive pro-West mandate from the Armenian people, sources confirmed to Euronews.

stories.title: Whoosh! Jacques Henri Lartigue’s world of colour – in pictures
stories.summary: The French artist is best known for his black-and-white images of high society, but a new exhibition shows us that he was also a bold and brilliant colour pioneer Continue reading...

stories.title: Europe’s June heatwave – in pictures
stories.summary: The ‘heat dome’ settling over western Europe could bring temperatures of up to 40C to some parts of England and Wales in the middle part of this week Continue reading...

stories.title: EU faces fierce criticism over plans to host Taliban in Brussels
stories.summary: Rights campaigners and MEPs say meeting would normalise regime that erases women from public life Rights campaigners and MEPs have warned that a meeting between EU officials and a Taliban delegation in Brussels risks normalising a regime that has banned girls from school beyond the sixth grade and sought to erase women from public life, while its ranks include two leaders accused of crimes against humanity. A spokesperson for the Afghan foreign ministry confirmed that a delegation representing t

stories.title: Europe battles record-breaking heat: is this the new normal? - The Latest
stories.summary: Europe is dealing with a debilitating heatwave, with schools closed, trains cancelled and France holding an emergency meeting after heat-related deaths. António Guterres, the UN chief, is urging the world to act on fossil fuels as the continent braces for record-breaking heat. Lucy Hough speaks to Europe environment correspondent Ajit Niranjan Continue reading...

stories.title: Germany's pension plans draw praise and outrage
stories.summary: A 33-point plan to reform Germany's pension system has drawn praise and criticism in almost equal measure. Centrist politicians have called it a good compromise, but opposition parties on the left and right are unhappy.
